dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses 'moving average line slopes upward/downward' which is interpreted as MA trending above/below recent price action. Used sma_20 as a reasonable intermediate MA period since specific period was AUTHOR_SILENT. Entry logic: bullish when MA slopes up (MA > prev close AND price rising); bearish when MA slopes down (MA < prev close AND price falling). Exit on slope reversal. The 'appropriate times' qualifier is captured by requiring price momentum confirmation.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses 'moving average line slopes upward/downward' which is interpreted as MA trending above/below recent price action. Used sma_20 as a reasonable intermediate MA period since specific period was AUTHOR_SILENT. Entry logic: bullish when MA slopes up (MA > prev close AND price rising); bearish when MA slopes down (MA < prev close AND price falling). Exit on slope reversal. The 'appropriate times' qualifier is captured by requiring price momentum confirmation.

source_rule_text: When the moving average line slopes upward, maintain a bullish bias and enter long positions at appropriate times; when the moving average line slopes downward, maintain a negative bias and enter short positions at appropriate times.
